Cursor Challenge01
AE Challenge · Topic 1 · Territory planning

Top 5 accounts in my segment

Ranked by technical population, strategic software initiatives, and how closely engineering work aligns with Cursor's strengths — not developer count alone. Engineering figures are territory estimates, not authoritative eligible-seat counts.

Prioritization framework
  • Technical population size
  • Strategic software initiatives
  • Alignment with Cursor's strengths
  • Win: integration, testing & AI features
1
Manhattan Associates
Supply chain / commerce SaaS
~1,800 devs (40%)~$1.1B rev4,500 emp
Who they are
  • Cloud subscription, license & services for supply chain SaaS
  • 3 straight years of YoY revenue growth
  • YoY: +21% · +12% · +4% (2023–25)

Makes money selling warehouse, transportation, and order management software — increasingly via cloud subscription on Manhattan Active, plus implementation services. Revenue has grown every year for the past three years: +21.1% in 2023 ($767M → $929M), +12.2% in 2024 ($1.04B), and +3.8% in 2025 ($1.08B) (NASDAQ: MANH).

2026 strategic priorities
  • Accelerate Active adoption
  • Grow cloud subscription ARR
  • Commercialize Active Agents & Agent Foundry
  • Ship faster without weakening security

Accelerate Active adoption across new logos and installed base; sustain cloud subscription growth; commercialize agentic AI through Active Agents and Agent Foundry; increase product velocity without weakening security or customer trust.

Where Cursor fits
  • Scores high on all three: seats, initiatives & fit
  • ~1,800 engineers, Active cloud/AI, PS builds integrations
  • Wins on integration work, testing & AI feature dev

I look at three things: the size of the technical population, the strategic software initiatives, and how closely engineering work aligns with Cursor's strengths. Manhattan ranked first because it scores high on all three — a big engineering org, active cloud and AI initiatives, and a large technical professional services group actually building integrations. Cursor wins by accelerating integration work, testing, and AI feature development.

Why #1 in my territory
  • Strongest total account thesis
  • High eligible-seat %
  • Clear tie to Manhattan Active & agentic AI

Strongest total account thesis — the best combination of seat potential, software intensity, and direct business impact. Manhattan is a product-software company: engineering velocity drives releases, implementations, subscription growth, and differentiation. ~1,800 estimated engineers is smaller than Verifone, but a high share are eligible Cursor users and the value story ties cleanly to Manhattan Active and agentic AI. Large microservices codebase, integrations, and frequent releases create strong use cases — and identifiable technology executives can connect developer productivity to the product roadmap.

2
Extreme Networks
Cloud networking
~1,600 devs (40%)~$1.3B rev3,955 emp
Who they are
  • Networking hardware, software subscriptions & support
  • Grew 2 of 3 years — FY24 dip after FY23 peak
  • YoY: +18% · −15% · +2% (FY23–25)

Makes money from enterprise networking hardware, software subscriptions, and services for cloud-managed LAN/WLAN (Platform ONE). Revenue grew +18% in FY23 ($1.31B) and +2% in FY25 ($1.14B), with a −15% reset in FY24 ($1.12B) after channel inventory normalization — not three consecutive growth years, but back on growth in FY25 (NASDAQ: EXTR).

2026 strategic priorities
  • Platform ONE GA
  • Agent ONE autonomous operations
  • Ship agentic AI faster than AIOps competitors

Platform ONE GA; Agent ONE for autonomous network operations; ship conversational, multimodal, and agentic AI features faster than legacy AIOps competitors. The AI-platform narrative gives Cursor a specific strategic initiative to support — not a generic productivity pitch.

Where Cursor fits
  • Strong #2 — smaller seat potential than Manhattan
  • Platform ONE engineers + support teams on services/APIs
  • Wins on integration work, testing & AI feature dev

Same three lenses — technical population, strategic initiatives, and engineering fit. Extreme is a strong number two, but with a smaller seat ceiling. The focus is Platform ONE's engineers and support teams integrating services and APIs across a complex networking stack. Cursor wins by accelerating integration work, testing, and AI feature development.

Why #2 in my territory
  • Best actionable “why now” account
  • Stronger probability-adjusted vs Verifone
  • Easier pilot, exec sponsor, roadmap tie-in

Strongest actionable “why Cursor, why now” account. Verifone has the larger theoretical opportunity; Extreme has the stronger probability-adjusted opportunity. Extreme appears more likely to engage around a strategic AI initiative, produce an executive sponsor, identify a concentrated pilot team, connect Cursor to an active product roadmap, and move through evaluation without requiring immediate global standardization.

3
Verifone
Payments technology
~2,200 devs (37%)~$1.8B+ rev5,964 emp
Who they are
  • Payment devices, gateways, processing & merchant services
  • Private — est. growth all 3 years (2022–24)
  • YoY (est.): +26% · +24% · +42%

Makes money through payment terminals, gateway/processing fees, merchant acquiring, and commerce platform services (~$1.8B+ revenue scale). Privately held since 2018, so figures are estimates — third-party sources show revenue up each of the last three years: ~$497M in 2022 (+26%), ~$615M in 2023 (+24%), ~$872M in 2024 (+42%). Continued investment signals expansion (e.g., $235M equity raise, Apr 2025).

2026 strategic priorities
  • Ship payment software at global scale
  • Expand Android POS & gateways
  • AI deeper in SDLC; hackathon culture

Ship payment software faster at global scale; expand Android POS and gateway capabilities; embed AI deeper into the SDLC. Public signals: Developer + AI Innovation hackathons and leadership stating AI is already deeply embedded in how they build.

Where Cursor fits
  • Largest technical population (~2,200) in the territory
  • Payment software scale + AI deeper in the SDLC
  • Integration-heavy POS/gateway work fits Cursor's strengths

Verifone leads on technical population size — ~2,200 engineers — with strategic pressure to ship payment software faster and embed AI in how they build. The work is integration-heavy: Android POS, gateways, merchant APIs, and certified payment apps. That maps cleanly to Cursor's strengths even if global rollout is harder. Land in the Bangalore AI Innovation Hub, prove value, then expand. Cursor wins by accelerating integration work, testing, and AI feature development.

Why #3 in my territory
  • Largest theoretical seat TAM (~2,200)
  • Harder convert & expand globally
  • Warm champion; slower mobilization than Extreme

Highest potential scale, more complex execution. ~2,200 estimated engineers gives Verifone the largest theoretical seat TAM in my territory. But global structure, hardware-plus-software mix, regional engineering organizations, payment-security requirements, and procurement complexity make it a harder account to convert and expand. Warm champion exists — ranked below Extreme because mobilization looks slower, not because upside is smaller long term.

4
Agilysys
Hospitality SaaS
~1,300 devs (48%)~$319M rev (FY26)2,687 emp
Who they are
  • Hospitality software subscriptions, maintenance & services
  • 3 straight years of YoY revenue growth
  • YoY: +22% · +20% · +16% (FY23–25)

Makes money from subscription & maintenance (~62% of revenue) plus hospitality software and professional services for hotels, resorts, casinos, and F&B. Three consecutive growth years: FY23 $198M (+22%), FY24 $237M (+20%), FY25 $276M (+16%). FY26 reached a record $319M (+16%) (NASDAQ: AGYS).

2026 strategic priorities
  • guestsense.ai — 30+ AI features
  • Agentic automation & revenue intelligence
  • CEO driving AI changes in R&D

guestsense.ai — 30+ AI features across multi-modal UX, hyper-personalization, agentic process automation, and revenue intelligence; new AI-native Revenue Intelligence and next-gen CRS modules filling ecosystem gaps. CEO is driving sweeping AI changes in R&D to improve operating leverage; FY27 revenue target $365–370M — ship cadence is the growth lever.

Where Cursor fits
  • Highest dev density (48%) — software is the business
  • guestsense.ai & agentic modules are the initiative
  • ~1,300-engineer India R&D ships the cloud roadmap

Agilysys scores well on initiative and fit — nearly half the company is engineering, and the bet is AI-native hospitality software (guestsense.ai, agentic automation, new modules). That is continuous integration, testing, and feature work across a large codebase — exactly where Cursor compounds. Cursor wins by accelerating integration work, testing, and AI feature development.

Why #4 in my territory
  • Excellent fit; smaller seat ceiling
  • ~1,300 seats vs top three
  • Highly winnable — not a weak fit

Highly winnable and relevant, but smaller seat ceiling. Highest developer density in my top five (48%) and a credible AI-native product bet — guestsense.ai, agentic automation, Marriott-scale deployments. Ranked fourth because total engineering population (~1,300) and contract ceiling trail Manhattan, Extreme, and Verifone. Not because the Cursor fit is weak — the fit is excellent; the absolute enterprise opportunity is smaller.

5
Guardian Life
Insurance / financial services
~1,200 devs (13%)~$16B rev9,500 emp
Who they are
  • Life, disability & dental premiums; annuities & investment income
  • 3 straight years of total revenue growth
  • YoY: +2% · +6% · +8% (2022–24)

Makes money from insurance premiums, annuity deposits, and net investment income across life, group benefits, and individual markets. Total revenue grew each of the past three years: $12.73B in 2022 (+2% vs 2021), $13.48B in 2023 (+6%), and $14.50B in 2024 (+8%) (Guardian Annual / financial highlights).

2026 strategic priorities
  • Legacy modernization at scale
  • Dev productivity on refactor work
  • Security & governance gate every decision

Legacy modernization at scale; digital transformation without weakening compliance; improve developer productivity on refactor-heavy work. Security, governance, and auditability gate every tooling decision before broad rollout.

Where Cursor fits
  • Weakest seat scale (13% dev density) of the five
  • Legacy modernization is the strategic software push
  • Refactor & test generation align with Cursor — with governance

Guardian is the outlier on population size — ~1,200 engineers supporting insurance, not shipping a product platform. The strategic initiative is legacy modernization under digital transformation: comprehending Java, .NET, and mainframe-adjacent systems and refactoring safely in a regulated environment. Cursor wins by accelerating integration work, testing, and AI-assisted development once security and architecture are in the room.

Why #5 in my territory
  • Large budget; weakest near-term profile
  • Only 13% dev density
  • Highest friction — not land-speed priority

Meaningful long-term transformation play, but highest friction and weakest near-term profile. Large budget and modernization potential, yet ~1,200 engineers are only 13% of ~9,500 employees — software supports the insurance business, it is not the core product. Security, privacy, AI governance, and procurement are more restrictive. Success likely requires a narrow modernization project with early security and architecture involvement — not where I optimize for land speed first.

How I'm prioritizing my territory

I look at three things: the size of the technical population, their strategic software initiatives, and how closely engineering work aligns with Cursor's strengths. The ranking is not developer count alone — Verifone has the largest technical population; Manhattan is #1 because it scores high on all three. Extreme is a strong #2 with smaller seat potential, focused on Platform ONE engineers integrating services and APIs. Cursor wins by accelerating integration work, testing, and AI feature development.

Sources: Cursor Accounts — Strategic Plan; public company and role research02 · TOPIC 1
AE Challenge · Topic 1 · Account context

Manhattan Associates: the 2026 account thesis

A cloud-native supply-chain software leader whose growth depends on converting more customers to Manhattan Active, expanding recurring cloud revenue, and turning AI innovation into differentiated customer outcomes.

Industry

Supply-chain commerce software

Manhattan builds enterprise software that coordinates inventory, fulfillment, transportation, warehouses, stores, and omnichannel orders.

  • Warehouse and transportation management
  • Order management and point of sale
  • Inventory optimization and unified commerce
How it makes money

Recurring cloud revenue plus implementation

  • Cloud subscriptions: The Manhattan Active suite—cloud-native applications for warehouse, labor, yard and transportation operations; order management, inventory visibility, fulfillment, point of sale and customer engagement; plus demand, allocation and replenishment planning.
  • Services: Engineers and solution consultants translate each customer’s operating model into production software. They integrate Manhattan with ERP, commerce, carrier, data and warehouse-automation systems; configure extensions; test complex workflows; troubleshoot go-live issues; and optimize performance after launch.
  • Maintenance: Recurring support for installed on-premises products and customer-specific integrations or extensions. The work includes defect fixes, security and compatibility updates, and lifecycle support for custom workflows and agents as business rules, APIs and connected systems change.
  • Licenses: Up-front perpetual fees for older on-premises software. This revenue is shrinking because new customers and migrations increasingly use multi-year Manhattan Active cloud subscriptions, where upgrades and support are included and revenue is recognized over the contract term.

Enterprise wins create durable recurring revenue and follow-on expansion.

Likely 2026 priorities

Scale the cloud platform and monetize innovation

  • Accelerate Manhattan Active adoption across new logos and the installed base.
  • Sustain cloud subscription growth while protecting delivery quality and margins.
  • Advance agentic AI through Active Maven, Active Agents, and Agent Foundry.
  • Increase product and services velocity without weakening security, reliability, or customer trust.
Cursor account thesis

Cursor can help Manhattan compress the path from product idea to secure, reviewed code across a large and complex engineering environment. The first conversation should focus on one measurable workflow—modernization, test creation, defect remediation, or agent development—and prove faster cycle time without compromising enterprise controls.

Sources: Manhattan Associates company and investor materials; public product portfolio and 2026 cloud/AI outlook. Logo asset: CDNLogo.00 · TOPIC 1
AE Challenge · Topic 1 · Account POV

Cursor POV

Business outcomes mapped to Manhattan's 2026 initiatives

Turn engineering velocity into enterprise value

Cursor fits where Manhattan's product, services, and platform teams convert cloud and agentic-AI strategy into secure production software. The POV links that work to the three outcomes an executive buying group will care about.

Increase revenue

Ship differentiated cloud and AI capabilities faster

  • Where Cursor fits: help product engineers understand large codebases, generate and refactor code, create tests, and move Active Agents and Agent Foundry concepts into reviewed production software faster.
  • Strategic link: accelerates Manhattan Active adoption, supports cloud-subscription growth, and gives sales teams more differentiated AI and commerce capabilities to take to new logos and the installed base.

Proof to measureShorter idea-to-production cycle time, faster agent delivery, and more roadmap capacity per release.

Decrease cost

Increase output across product and customer delivery

  • Where Cursor fits: reduce repetitive coding, test creation, documentation, integration work, defect diagnosis, and legacy-code navigation for both product engineering and services teams.
  • Strategic link: improves implementation and maintenance productivity, protects services margins, and helps Manhattan scale cloud migrations and customer-specific work without adding headcount at the same rate.

Proof to measureEngineering hours saved, lower cost per resolved issue, faster implementation milestones, and reduced rework.

Decrease risk

Modernize and innovate without weakening trust

  • Where Cursor fits: support policy-aware code generation, broader test coverage, safer refactoring, faster vulnerability and defect remediation, and consistent review workflows across complex repositories.
  • Strategic link: protects reliability, security, and customer trust as Manhattan expands Manhattan Active, retires legacy components, and introduces customer-facing agents and AI-driven workflows.

Proof to measureDefect escape rate, remediation time, test coverage, review time, and policy-compliant code acceptance.

Cursor POV mapped to Manhattan Associates' cloud, AI, product, services, security, and margin priorities from the account brief.01 · TOPIC 1

One account · five paths into the buying committee

The selection covers the full enterprise motion: vision, deployment, R&D velocity, operational adoption, and a practical first use case.

Selection logic: cover the decision chain from technical strategy through R&D scale, enterprise operations, and hands-on adoption.02 · TOPIC 2
01 · Executive sponsor

Sanjeev sets the technology standard

Target role · vision & budgetSanjeev SiotiaExecutive Vice President & Chief Technology Officer

Owns Manhattan’s technology strategy and the architecture behind its cloud-native, API-first product platform.

Why I’m reaching out: he can make Cursor a strategic engineering standard—not a scattered team-level tool—and connect it directly to Manhattan’s agentic-AI roadmap.
Priority 01Commercialize agentic AI

Move from assistants to agents that can safely act inside warehouse, transportation, and omnichannel workflows.

Priority 02Protect architecture quality

Keep human guardrails, unified data, and reliable execution as AI-generated software accelerates.

Priority 03Accelerate cloud innovation

Increase R&D velocity while Manhattan converts on-prem customers and sustains cloud subscription growth.

›

Opening thesis: Cursor can help Manhattan’s engineers ship Agent Foundry and Manhattan Active capabilities faster while preserving the architectural standards and review discipline Sanjeev requires.

Sources: supplied Sales Navigator profile; Manhattan “Architecture of Action” and NRF 2026 agentic-AI commentary.03 · TOPIC 2
02 · Platform buyer

Jonathan can operationalize enterprise adoption

Target role · deployment & governanceJonathan CoxVice President & Chief Information Officer

Leads enterprise IT infrastructure and operations—the environment where identity, access, procurement, support, and developer-tool standardization become real.

Why I’m reaching out: Jonathan is the likely operational buyer who can turn CTO sponsorship into a secure, supportable, globally governed rollout.
Priority 01Reliable global operations

Keep infrastructure resilient as cloud products, services delivery, and customer expectations scale.

Priority 02Control the tool estate

Standardize identity, provisioning, access, support, and spend instead of allowing unmanaged AI-tool sprawl.

Priority 03Enable measurable adoption

Give leadership usage analytics and a clear view of whether AI tools improve engineering outcomes.

›

Opening thesis: Cursor Enterprise gives Jonathan the SSO, SCIM, role controls, repo/model/MCP policies, and adoption analytics needed to scale AI coding without losing operational control.

Sources: TAG and Kennesaw advisory-board biographies; Cursor Enterprise identity, admin-control, and analytics documentation.04 · TOPIC 2
03 · R&D leader

Kartik multiplies engineering throughput at scale

Target role · R&D velocityKartik PandyaVice President, Engineering R&D

Leads Manhattan's Engineering R&D organization—the teams designing, building, and shipping Manhattan Active across the containerized, microservices platform he helped shape.

Why I'm reaching out: Kartik drove the transformation from a 10M-line monolith to 250+ microservices and evangelized DevOps across 800+ developers. As technical counsel to the CTO, he owns the engineering velocity behind Manhattan's cloud ramp — Cursor maps directly to that mandate.
Priority 01Scale cloud-native delivery

Keep release velocity high across microservices as Manhattan Active expands to new modules, customers, and agentic-AI capabilities.

Priority 02Raise developer productivity

Multiply engineering output across distributed R&D teams without proportional headcount growth — a cost-effective lever as cloud subscription revenue ramps.

Priority 03Modernize without losing discipline

Preserve 12-factor, interface-first, and review standards as teams adopt AI-assisted development at enterprise scale.

›

Opening thesis: Cursor gives R&D teams codebase-aware acceleration on Manhattan's microservices estate — faster Active releases and AI feature delivery while preserving the architectural discipline Kartik has spent years instilling.

Sources: supplied LinkedIn / Sales Navigator profile; public role records; Manhattan cloud and microservices architecture context.05 · TOPIC 2
04 · Scale champion

Udai turns product velocity into customer outcomes

Target role · services & adoptionUdai TennatiSVP, Technology Services · SVP, Customer Experience Organization, India

Leads teams responsible for implementation, technical services, customer experience, and the delivery capacity that converts subscriptions into successful go-lives.

Why I’m reaching out: Udai owns a high-volume environment where faster debugging, configuration, integration work, and onboarding can produce visible customer and margin impact.
Priority 01Faster time-to-value

Reduce implementation friction and get customers live on Manhattan Active and new AI capabilities sooner.

Priority 02Scale delivery quality

Help distributed services teams solve issues consistently without relying on a small group of experts.

Priority 03Grow cloud retention

Strengthen adoption, renewals, and expansion by connecting engineering speed to customer outcomes.

›

Opening thesis: Pilot Cursor with an implementation-engineering pod and measure onboarding time, defect resolution, integration throughput, and reusable delivery accelerators.

Sources: supplied Sales Navigator profile; public customer recommendations; Manhattan cloud-conversion and services strategy.06 · TOPIC 2
05 · Pilot champion

Ashish is the practical path to first value

Target role · use case & proofAshish AndotraDirector, AI & F&A · Digital Transformation, Enterprise Applications, Data & Analytics

Sits at the intersection of AI, finance workflows, enterprise applications, data, and measurable digital-transformation outcomes.

Why I’m reaching out: Ashish is close enough to the work to identify a high-value pilot, but senior enough to connect the result to enterprise transformation and ROI.
Priority 01Turn AI into operating leverage

Automate finance and enterprise-application workflows instead of adding another isolated experimentation tool.

Priority 02Ship trusted internal tools

Accelerate prototypes and production changes while preserving data, architecture, and review standards.

Priority 03Prove measurable value

Connect AI adoption to cycle time, backlog reduction, quality, and business-process outcomes.

›

Opening thesis: Co-design a narrow pilot—an internal finance or enterprise-app workflow—and use Cursor to move from requirements to tested code quickly, with measurable before-and-after results.

AE Challenge · Topic 3 · Sample messaging

Respond to Sanjeev's post with a ready-to-send note

Yellow highlights mark the post lines we're pulling into the note — including model agility. Click the post or button to open the message.

SS
Sanjeev SiotiaEVP, Chief Technology Officer at Manhattan Associates2mo · Public

“Vintage is a lot more than just the grapes.”

Last week I had the privilege of joining Sally Miller, CIO of DHL Supply Chain, and her team in Florence for a wine tasting. The discussion was about what it actually takes to operationalize AI in real-world production.

Enterprise leaders need to navigate the fast-paced AI transition. The seductive narrative is that AI generates the grapes (the code). Because code generation is becoming cheap, people think they can instantly make their own “wine.”

But the real enterprise value in this transition comes from the process — fermentation, aging, consistency, and craftsmanship.

Don't confuse the ability to generate code with the ability to deliver a business outcome.

THINK: The Agility of the Brain

Model Agility: Is your architecture hard-coded to a single model provider, or can you switch models dynamically based on the task?

SEE: The Gravity of Data

Security Beyond ACLs: How do you control the context the AI sees?

DO: The Deterministic Spine

Deterministic Execution: Do the AI's actions go through a pre-validated, API-driven execution layer with guardrails?

The market is obsessed with the grapes — the latest model, the fastest inference, the shiny demo. But in the enterprise, the vintage is what matters.

Are you buying grapes, or are you building a vintage?

Yellow = key lines from the post (including model agility)

What they're building

Manhattan Active is the cloud-native platform for warehouse, transportation, and order management. Active Agents (GA Jan 2026) embed autonomous AI inside that platform — agents that act on live operational data, not a bolt-on layer. Agent Foundry lets customers and developers build and customize those agents in plain language or with APIs — the factory behind Manhattan's agentic AI bet.

Tied to his technology priorities

  • Commercialize agentic AI: Ship ready-to-work Active Agents and help customers build their own in Agent Foundry — labor, wave, dock, and workflow automation inside Manhattan Active.
  • Protect architecture quality: Agents run with guardrails, approved APIs, and auditability as engineering speed increases.
  • Accelerate cloud innovation: More R&D throughput on Manhattan Active and agent capabilities while the installed base moves to cloud.

Research links

  • Manhattan Active
  • Manhattan Active Agents
  • Agent Foundry

Ask: 30-minute working conversation — your POV on how Cursor supports these, not a slide deck.

New message
SS
Sanjeev SiotiaEVP, Chief Technology Officer at Manhattan Associates

Hi Sanjeev,

I just finished reading your post on operationalizing AI in the real world.

It seems like your biggest focus is on using AI effectively to help deliver results for your customers with Manhattan Active and Agent Foundry. The two biggest points I noticed in the post were the importance of staying model agnostic and not getting locked into a single LLM, and the need for AI to generate real business outcomes through understanding the codebase context end to end.

I'd love 30 minutes to walk through my point of view on how Cursor could help your engineering org ship that roadmap faster — and hear how you're thinking about productivity across R&D.

Would you be open to finding time to connect next week?

Best,
Ryan

Preview — copy into LinkedIn to send
Sources: Sanjeev Siotia LinkedIn post; Manhattan Active Platform, Active Agents (Jan 2026 GA), and Agent Foundry product pages (manh.com)11 · TOPIC 3
